Let $f : \mathbb{Z} \times \mathbb{Z} \to \mathbb{R}$ be a function which
satisfies $f(0, 0) = 1$ and
$$ f(m, n) + f(m + 1, n) + f(m, n + 1) + f(m + 1, n + 1) = 0 $$
for all $m, n \in \mathbb{Z}$ (where $\mathbb{Z}$ and $\mathbb{R}$ denote the
set of all integers and all real numbers, respectively). Prove that $|f(m, n)|
\ge 1/3$, for infinitely many pairs of integers $(m, n)$.
